title: Tanker report - Week 47 2015
summary:
VLCCs
A slower week mainly due to a large percentage of the main players being in Dubai. Rates for Middle Eeast Gulf basis 270,000 tonnes cargo drifted down gently with WS 63 being last paid for a short run to Thailand and long east is evaluated now at around WS 61/62 level. Going west, Bahri took Athenian tonnage for 280,000 tonnes to the US Gulf at WS 36 cape/cape at the start of the week.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: A slower week mainly due to a large percentage of the main players being in Dubai. Rates for Middle Eeast Gulf basis 270,000 tonnes cargo drifted down gently with WS 63 being last paid for a short run to Thailand and long east is evaluated now at around WS 61/62 level.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: The status quo was generally maintained in West Africa with rates steady at WS 80. By contrast the increasing Turkish straits delays (some charterers expecting around 10 days transit time) has seen charterers reach out for loading as far ahead as 16/18 December.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: The market has remained firm with rates from Sidi Kerir/Ceyhan hovering around WS 115/117.5 level. An active week from the Black Sea, with the inherent straits delays has seen rates nudge up further with both WS 117.5 and WS 120 being done here.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: Off the Continent , the market for 55,000 tonnes to the US Gulf has firmed further to around WS 140, as a result of the strong Caribbean market (which has risen to high WS 180s) enticing tonnage to stay local rather than ballast across.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: Rates for 37,000 tonnes from Continent to USAC started the week at WS 135 but a lack of enquiry combined with an excessive tonnage list has seen rates fall by around WS10. The backhaul market for 38,000 tonnes was more active due to better levels of enquiry for short haul trips to Mexico/central America which quickly reduced the tonnage list and rates rose around WS 10 to the WS 120 level.
